name: Cornell Chicken
description: From Meathead: The Science of Great Barbecue and Grilling (Goldwyn), p.298.
recipeInstructions: Whisk the egg, oil, vinegar, salt, S&G rub, and pepper into a marinade; marinate the chicken quarters 3-24 hours.
Grill indirect (~325F), flipping, until breasts hit 160F and thighs 165F.
author: Meathead Goldwyn

ai.cliffNotes: An emulsified vinegar-oil marinade with egg creates a basting liquid that keeps chicken moist during indirect grilling while building flavor.
keyTips: Marinate 3-24 hours for the acidity to penetrate and tenderize, but don't exceed 24 hours or the texture becomes mushy
Use indirect heat at 325°F to avoid burning the oil-based marinade while allowing even cooking
Monitor internal temps carefully (160°F breast, 165°F thigh) since the marinade and flipping can mask doneness visually
Reserve some marinade before adding raw chicken to use as a finishing baste in the final minutes of grilling
